message:
feat(wezterm+simulation): tick 203 MockWezterm::add_pane_with_cx + setup_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Scenario::setup_with_cx at simulation.rs L651 was calling ambient
`mock.add_pane(pane).await` in a cx-first setup loop. The loop
already threaded cx via per-iteration checkpoints, but the actual
pane-insert was using MockWezterm::add_pane which took the panes
RwLock via plain `write().await` — picking up cx via
Cx::current() thread-local fallback instead of the explicit cx.

Tick 203 is a 2-part fix:

1. wezterm.rs: add MockWezterm::add_pane_with_cx(cx, pane) as the
   cx-first sibling of add_pane. Routes the write-lock wait
   through write_with_cx(cx). Matches the pattern of inject_with_cx
   (L6815) added earlier and used by execute_until_with_cx (tick
   202).

2. simulation.rs: Scenario::setup_with_cx now calls
   add_pane_with_cx(cx, pane) instead of add_pane(pane). The
   write-lock wait now honors the caller's explicit cx on cancel.

Legacy add_pane (L6751) and legacy setup (no cx variant) are
unchanged for ambient-cx callers and non-asupersync builds.

Verification:
- 185 / 185 simulation:: tests pass under --features asupersync-runtime
- 171 / 171 wezterm:: tests pass under --features asupersync-runtime
via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
